metadata.title: Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Steroid Contraceptives in Women

rules.rule_text: Contraceptives used by healthy individuals for prophylactic/preventive purposes must have a very low risk and favourable risk/benefit balance. A well-defined contraceptive efficacy and well-founded description of risks and adverse events are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Contraceptives are generally used by healthy individuals for prophylactic/preventive purposes. They therefore need to have a very low risk in order to have a favourable risk/benefit balance. They need to have a well-defined contraceptive efficacy and a well-founded description of risks and adverse events to enable the woman and the prescriber to make the best individual choice of contraceptive method.

rules.rule_text: The nature and hormonal activity of the steroid(s) and principal metabolites must be described, including receptor binding studies, individual metabolite studies, and indicators of hormonal activity such as SHBG (sex hormone binding globulin).
rules.verbatim_quote: The nature and hormonal activity of the steroid(s) and its (their) principal metabolites should be described. Such information may come from receptor binding studies, studies of individual metabolites in animals (and/or man) and studies of indicators of hormonal activity, such as SHBG (sex hormone binding globulin).

rules.rule_text: Effects on ovarian function must be described in women with normal ovulatory function using plasma concentrations of ovarian steroids, gonadotrophins, and ultrasound of ovaries. At least two cycles must be studied in each woman.
rules.verbatim_quote: The effects on ovarian function in women with normal ovulatory function should be described. Methods to be used include plasma concentrations of ovarian steroids and gonadotrophins and ultrasound of ovaries. At least two cycles should be studied in each woman.

rules.rule_text: Sampling frequency must be sufficient to detect LH peaks, ovulations, and duration of luteal phase or estrogen peaks. For entirely new steroids or combinations, comparative studies using a method with similar mechanism of action and dosage form are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important that sampling is so frequent that possible LH (luteinising hormone) peaks or ovulations are not missed and the duration of the luteal phase or estrogen peaks can be registered. For an entirely new contraceptive steroid or combination of steroids, comparative studies using a method with similar mechanism of action and dosage form should be made.

rules.rule_text: For long-acting contraceptives (implants), pharmacodynamic data must be provided at intervals for the entire period of intended use.
rules.verbatim_quote: For long-acting contraceptives (e.g. implants) this kind of data should be provided at intervals for the entire period of intended use.

rules.rule_text: Time to onset of action, time to return of normal ovarian function after discontinuation, and time to return of fertility must be studied. Return of fertility must be followed for up to a year in all patients discontinuing treatment for wish of pregnancy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The time to onset of action, in relation to start of treatment and dose of steroids and the time to return of normal ovarian function after discontinuation of treatment should be studied in a sufficient number of patients... The time to return of fertility should be followed for up to a year in all patients discontinuing treatment for wish of pregnancy.

rules.rule_text: Effects on other endocrine systems (hypothalamic-pituitary, adrenal, thyroid) in women must be described.
rules.verbatim_quote: The effects on other endocrine systems (hypothalamic-pituitary, adrenal, thyroid) in woman should be described.
rules.exceptions: May not be necessary for minor dose/regimen modifications of well-known steroids with well-documented effects

rules.rule_text: Haemostatic variables, plasma lipids, and carbohydrate metabolism must be studied using modern state-of-the-art methods, with relevant comparator(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: The effects on haemostatic variables, plasma lipids and carbohydrate metabolism should be studied with modern, state of the art methods (see also Safety section). Studies should include relevant comparator(s).

rules.rule_text: For products suppressing ovarian estrogen secretion (without containing estrogen), effects on bone mineral density and/or bone metabolism must be studied with validated methods.
rules.verbatim_quote: For products not containing an estrogen and suppressing estrogen secretion from the ovaries, the effect on bone mineral density and/or bone metabolism should be studied with validated methods.

rules.rule_text: Key studies must be large enough to yield a Pearl Index (pregnancies per 100 woman-years) with a two-sided 95% CI where the difference between upper limit and point estimate does not exceed 1, and the point estimate does not exceed 1.
rules.source_section: 3.1 Study size requirements and pregnancy reporting
rules.verbatim_quote: The key studies, carried out in a sufficiently representative population, should normally be at least large enough to give the overall Pearl Index (number of pregnancies per 100 woman years) with a two-sided 95% confidence interval such that the difference between the upper limit of the confidence interval and the point estimate does not exceed 1 (pregnancies per 100 woman years).

rules.rule_text: Pregnancy rates must be described by both Pearl Index and life table analysis, including all pregnancies during treatment. Pregnancies after premature discontinuation (within 3 months) must also be reported.
rules.source_section: 3.1 Study size requirements and pregnancy reporting
rules.verbatim_quote: Pregnancy rates should be described by Pearl Index and life table analysis including all pregnancies during treatment. Pregnancies following premature discontinuation of study medication should also be included in the calculations... Pregnancies within three months following premature discontinuation of study medication should also be reported.
rules.exceptions: Excluded only if date of conception determined by valid method (ultrasound, beta-HCG) is clearly after premature discontinuation

rules.rule_text: The Pearl Index for method failure must be calculated separately, requiring reliable compliance recording methods (e.g., electronic patient diaries) to exclude non-compliers from the denominator.
rules.source_section: 3.1 Study size requirements and pregnancy reporting
rules.verbatim_quote: The separate calculation of the Pearl Index for method failure requires reliable methods for recording of compliance (e.g. electronic patient diaries) not to include non-compliers in the denominator.

rules.rule_text: For Pearl Index calculations, 13 cycles of 28 days constitute one woman-year. For non-cyclic methods, one woman-year equals one calendar year.
rules.source_section: 3.1 Study size requirements and pregnancy reporting
rules.verbatim_quote: For Pearl index calculations, 13/28-day cycles constitute one woman year. With non-cyclic methods one woman year equals one calendar year.

rules.rule_text: Efficacy study duration must be 6 months to 1 year or more. At least 400 women must complete one year of treatment. For long-acting products, study duration must cover the claimed duration of effectiveness with at least 200 women completing the claimed duration of use (for products >3 years).
rules.verbatim_quote: The duration of efficacy studies should be six months to one year or more. For any new contraceptive, at least 400 women should have completed one year of treatment. For long-acting products (e.g. implants, medicated IUDs) the study duration should cover the claimed duration of effectiveness. For long-acting products, intended to be used for more than three years, the number of women completing the claimed duration of use should be at least 200.

rules.rule_text: Demography of study participants must be carefully described including weight, height, BMI, age, education, sexual relation/activity, parity, smoking, alcohol use, menstrual-related symptoms, and condom use. Where heterogeneity of fertility exists, separate Pearl Index estimates must be presented for important subgroups.
rules.source_section: 3.4 Demography of women included in studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The demography of the group of women included in studies should be carefully described, especially regarding factors thought to be relevant for the overall contraceptive efficacy of the method (e.g. weight, height, BMI, age, education, sexual relation/activity, parity, smoking, alcohol use, menstrual related symptoms, concomitant use of condoms to protect from sexually transmissible disease etc.). Where heterogeneity of fertility is likely... separate estimates or specific studies of the Pearl Index should be presented for important subgroups.

rules.rule_text: Comparative efficacy studies with active comparator are generally not required. However, for new products with mechanisms not consistently inhibiting ovulation (potentially higher pregnancy rates, PI>1), comparative studies may be necessary.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies including an active comparator are not generally requested for efficacy purposes. For a new product utilising a mechanism of action which may result in a relatively high pregnancy rate (PI>1), comparative studies may be necessary.
rules.exceptions: Pharmacodynamic data may provide advisory information on the necessity for comparative studies

rules.rule_text: Minimum safety information must come from studies including at least 400 women completing one year of treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: For any new steroid contraceptive the minimum amount of safety information should come from studies including at least 400 women completing one year of treatment.

rules.rule_text: For new combined oestrogen-progestogen contraceptive products, biological variables possibly related to VTE risk must be investigated using crossover design biomarker comparative studies against established comparators (levonorgestrel + ethinylestradiol 150/30mcg or desogestrel + ethinylestradiol 150/30mcg). Variables include prothrombin fragment 1+2, APC resistance, d-dimer, factor VII, factor VIII, factor II, antithrombin, protein S, protein C, and SHBG.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biological variables that may reflect different pharmacological effects, possibly related to VTE risk, should be investigated in the development of a new combined (oestrogen-progestogen) contraceptive product. Variables suggesting such different pharmacological effects may include prothrombin fragment 1+2, APC resistance (ETP-based, APTT-based), d-dimer, factor VII, factor VIII, factor II, antithrombin, protein S, protein C and SHBG.

rules.rule_text: Bleeding events must be analysed with cycle control analyses and also with analyses of bleeding/spotting during reference periods (e.g., 90 days) in accordance with WHO recommendations. Safety comparator should be chosen from market-leading products with similar mechanism of action and schedule.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Other adverse events, need for comparative studies
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to cycle control analyses with respect to bleeding and spotting, analyses of bleeding control during a reference period are advised, e.g. for periods of 90 days and in accordance with WHO recommendations. The comparator should, whenever possible, be chosen among market leading products with a similar mechanism of action and schedule of use.
rules.exceptions: When dose of existing product is reduced, comparison with higher-dosed product should also be made

rules.rule_text: All pregnancies occurring during a trial must be followed up for final outcome (mother and child) to complement preclinical teratogenicity studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: All pregnancies occurring during a trial should be followed up for final outcome (mother and child). This will provide complementary information to preclinical studies in animals.
